howItWorks.intro: This retirement income calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Monthly from Savings = Total Savings × Withdrawal Rate / 12
Total Monthly = Savings Withdrawal + Social Security + Pension + Other
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: How much do I need for retirement?
faq.answer: A common target is 80% of pre-retirement income. With Social Security covering about 40%, your savings need to generate the other 40%. The exact amount depends on your lifestyle.

faq.question: What is a safe withdrawal rate?
faq.answer: The 4% rule is the most commonly cited: withdraw 4% of your portfolio in year one, then adjust for inflation. Some financial planners now recommend 3.5% for greater safety.

faq.question: When should I claim Social Security?
faq.answer: You can claim at 62 (reduced benefit), full retirement age (66-67), or 70 (maximum benefit — 24-32% higher). Each year you delay past FRA adds about 8%.

faq.question: Will my savings last 30 years?
faq.answer: At a 4% withdrawal rate with a diversified portfolio, historical data shows a 95%+ success rate over 30 years. Lower withdrawal rates increase certainty.

howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This retirement income calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable calculations without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.

When comparing different financial products or scenarios side by side
When building a budget or projecting future savings and expenses
